title: Shashanth Sriramanathan
text: I am PhD candidate at the University of Bristol, supervised by Dr. Andy Young. I work on modelling the innermost regions of the accretion flow in Active Galactic Nuclei, both in the spectral and time domain. I am also a part of the TALES doctoral network. I am broadly interested in anything to do with accretion physics in compact objects, ranging from X-ray binaries to AGN.

title: About
text:
I am a PhD candidate at the University of Bristol, working under the supervision of Andy Young. I use X-ray emission to study the innermost regions of Active Galactic Nuclei (AGN), focussing on applying and developing spectral-timing models. I am also an active member of the doctoral network TALES, which was established to use time-domain astronomy techniques to study several aspects of AGN.
Previously, I completed a master’s thesis at the Anton Pannekoek Institute, University of Amsterdam, under the supervision of Phil Uttley - I worked on using the bispectrum, a second order Fourier method, to study quasi-periodic oscillations in X-ray binaries.

title: Research
text:
Studying Quasi-Periodic Oscillations using the bispectrum.
